refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 (Refined): The NGHE solution shall enable achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target, measured as energy_per_ton ≤ E_target, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and operational feedback mechanisms across the vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms by which efficiency is achieved. Refinement clarifies that efficiency is a system-level property emerging from coordinated subsystem behaviors: vehicle propulsion efficiency, teleoperation latency/command fidelity, and safety-driven operational constraints. This decomposition recognizes that energy efficiency is not a single design parameter but a composite outcome requiring measurement, feedback, and cross-subsystem optimization.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton over the agreed evaluation period under representative worksite conditions. Verify that integrated monitoring (telemetry collection), control optimization (command execution fidelity), and safety compliance (no efficiency-safety trade-off violations) collectively satisfy the target. Acceptance: measured energy_per_ton ≤ E_target with documented evidence of subsystem contributions.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report energy consumption and work output (tons moved) via telemetry.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall optimize vehicle propulsion and control to minimize energy per unit work while maintaining safety constraints.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall provide operational feedback to the operator (local or tele-operator) to enable energy-aware decision-making.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall ensure that safety-critical operations do not degrade energy efficiency through unnecessary energy waste or inefficient safe-state transitions.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a closed-loop energy management system with real-time telemetry collection, predictive efficiency modeling, and adaptive control algorithms that adjust vehicle propulsion parameters (throttle, gear selection, load distribution) based on worksite conditions and operator commands.
DP-B: Implement a passive energy monitoring and reporting system that logs energy consumption and work output post-operation, with operator guidance based on historical efficiency trends.

DP-C: Implement a hybrid approach combining real-time telemetry and efficiency monitoring (DP-A core) with safety-integrated constraints that prevent efficiency optimization from violating safety policies, and operator feedback mechanisms (DP-A + safety gating).
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a hybrid closed-loop energy management system that integrates real-time telemetry collection, predictive efficiency modeling, and adaptive control algorithms, with explicit safety-constraint gating and operator feedback mechanisms. The system shall continuously monitor energy consumption and work output, optimize vehicle propulsion parameters (throttle, gear selection, load distribution) based on worksite conditions and operator commands, and provide feedback to the operator while ensuring that no efficiency optimization violates safety policies or operational constraints.

potential_sources: Energy optimization logic is coupled to vehicle propulsion control (expected and necessary).
Safety constraint gating introduces coupling between Safety Services and energy optimization (acceptable; safety is non-negotiable).
Teleoperation introduces coupling via command latency and fidelity (acceptable; tele-operator must receive timely feedback).
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits acceptable coupling: the dependencies are functional necessities (vehicle control, safety gating, operator feedback) rather than architectural defects. The design avoids unnecessary coupling by separating monitoring (telemetry) from optimization (control) and from safety enforcement (constraint gating). Decoupling risk is low if interfaces are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Vehicle propulsion system supports adaptive parameter control (throttle, gear, load distribution) without introducing safety hazards.
ASSUMPTION: Telemetry infrastructure (sensors, network) can reliably report energy consumption and work output with sufficient fidelity for real-time optimization.
ASSUMPTION: Safety Services module can provide constraint definitions and real-time gating without introducing unacceptable latency.
RISK: If telemetry latency exceeds operator reaction time, feedback may be ineffective or misleading.
RISK: If safety constraint gating is overly conservative, efficiency gains may be negated.
RISK: If adaptive control algorithms are not validated for all worksite conditions, optimization may fail or cause unintended behavior.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is sound but should be refined to explicitly specify: (1) telemetry update frequency and latency requirements, (2) safety constraint interface specification (what constraints, how enforced), (3) operator feedback modality and timing, and (4) validation scope for adaptive control algorithms. These refinements should be captured in sub-DP specifications at the next decomposition level.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the energy management system logic, including telemetry aggregation, efficiency modeling, adaptive control coordination, and safety-constraint gating. The module is responsible for ensuring that energy_per_ton ≤ E_target is achieved through coordinated subsystem operation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional telemetry interface: NGHE Solution receives energy consumption, work output, and vehicle state data from Vehicle subsystem; sends efficiency feedback and optimization parameters to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives operator commands (local or tele-operator) via Tasking interface; translates commands into vehicle control directives and efficiency-optimized execution plans.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services; sends efficiency optimization status and constraint compliance confirmations to Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to coordinate with Teleoperation subsystem for tele-operator feedback and command transmission; also supports remote monitoring and diagnostics.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of energy management logic. It integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. High containment: energy management is a system-level concern that must coordinate across subsystems. Low diffusion: logic is centralized in the NGHE Solution module with clear interfaces to subsystems.

Vehicle could own propulsion optimization and telemetry collection, but energy management logic (modeling, feedback, constraint gating) would be diffused across Vehicle and NGHE Solution. Not recommended as primary owner; Vehicle should own propulsion control and telemetry generation, not system-level efficiency management.

Teleoperation could own operator feedback mechanisms, but energy management logic and vehicle control coordination would be diffused. Not recommended; Teleoperation should own tele-operator interface and command transmission, not energy management.

Safety Services could own constraint gating, but energy optimization logic and telemetry aggregation would be diffused. Not recommended; Safety Services should own safety policy enforcement, not energy management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) energy efficiency is a system-level KPI that emerges from coordinated subsystem behavior, (2) energy management logic (monitoring, modeling, optimization, feedback) is inherently cross-subsystem and should not be fragmented, (3) NGHE Solution already integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, making it the natural locus for system-level concerns, (4) containment score of 95 reflects minimal diffusion risk.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic module, it may accumulate too many responsibilities (energy management, safety gating, teleoperation coordination). Mitigation: decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules (Energy Management, Safety Coordinator, Teleoperation Coordinator) at the next level.

RISK: If interfaces between NGHE Solution and subsystems are poorly defined, changes to subsystem behavior may require rework in NGHE Solution. Mitigation: establish clear, versioned interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with explicit data schemas and timing requirements.

RISK: If telemetry latency or Safety Services constraint gating introduces unacceptable delays, energy optimization may be ineffective. Mitigation: specify latency budgets and validate end-to-end timing at integration testing.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary. Recommended refinements: (1) decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules (Energy Management, Safety Coordinator, Teleoperation Coordinator) to reduce monolithic risk, (2) formalize interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link with explicit data schemas, timing requirements, and error handling, (3) establish clear ownership of telemetry aggregation, efficiency modeling, and adaptive control logic within NGHE Solution sub-modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid closed-loop energy management with safety gating) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve energy_per_ton ≤ E_target through integrated monitoring, optimization, and feedback). The DP specifies the mechanisms (telemetry, predictive modeling, adaptive control, safety gating, operator feedback) that enable the FR outcome. Coupling is acceptable (vehicle control, safety constraints, teleoperation feedback are functional necessities). No revision needed.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (Low): Energy management logic is centralized in NGHE Solution, but implementation may require coordination with Vehicle (propulsion control), Teleoperation (operator feedback), and Safety Services (constraint gating). Mitigation: establish clear sub-module boundaries and interface contracts.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary with containment score of 95. Energy management logic is not diffused across multiple modules; instead, it is integrated at the NGHE Solution level with clear interfaces to subsystems. Ownership is unambiguous: NGHE Solution owns energy management, Vehicle owns propulsion, Teleoperation owns operator interface, Safety Services owns safety policy.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: measure energy_per_ton over the agreed evaluation period under representative worksite conditions. Acceptance criteria are clear (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target). Traceability is clear: FR -> DP (energy management system) -> IM (NGHE Solution) -> Verification (measurement and analysis).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns energy management system; Vehicle owns propulsion control and telemetry generation; Teleoperation owns operator feedback; Safety Services owns constraint gating. Interfaces are defined (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No ambiguity in responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Formalize interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link with explicit data schemas, timing requirements, and error handling.
Decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules (Energy Management, Safety Coordinator, Teleoperation Coordinator) to reduce monolithic risk and clarify internal responsibilities.
Specify telemetry update frequency, latency budgets, and safety constraint gating latency to ensure real-time optimization is feasible.
Define validation scope for adaptive control algorithms (worksite conditions, edge cases, failure modes).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 has been successfully refined into a coherent FR-DP-IM chain. The refined FR is clear (achieve energy_per_ton ≤ E_target through integrated monitoring, optimization, and feedback), the selected DP is sound (hybrid closed-loop energy management with safety gating), and the assigned IM is appropriate (NGHE Solution as the top-level owner). However, the refined FR is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (telemetry monitoring, propulsion optimization, operator feedback, safety-efficiency integration). To ensure complete functional decomposition, we must decompose each sub-FR into its own FR-DP-IM chain at the next level. This is a ZIGZAG_DOWN decision: proceed to the next decomposition level for each sub-FR.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR explicitly lists four sub-FRs that are not yet decomposed. Each sub-FR requires its own DP and IM assignment to ensure complete coverage and clear ownership. For example, CR-NGHE-003.1 (telemetry monitoring) requires a DP specifying sensor selection, data aggregation, and transmission, and an IM assigning ownership to Vehicle or NGHE Solution. Similarly, CR-NGHE-003.2 (propulsion optimization) requires a DP specifying control algorithms and an IM assigning ownership to Vehicle or NGHE Solution. Proceeding to the next level ensures that all sub-FRs are fully decomposed and that no functional gaps remain.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report energy consumption and work output (tons moved) via telemetry.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall optimize vehicle propulsion and control to minimize energy per unit work while maintaining safety constraints.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall provide operational feedback to the operator (local or tele-operator) to enable energy-aware decision-making.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall ensure that safety-critical operations do not degrade energy efficiency through unnecessary energy waste or inefficient safe-state transitions.

assumptions: Vehicle propulsion system supports adaptive parameter control (throttle, gear, load distribution) without introducing safety hazards.
Telemetry infrastructure (sensors, network) can reliably report energy consumption and work output with sufficient fidelity for real-time optimization.
Safety Services module can provide constraint definitions and real-time gating without introducing unacceptable latency.
Operator (local or tele-operator) can receive and act on efficiency feedback in a timely manner.
Energy efficiency target (E_target) is defined by the customer and is achievable with the NGHE solution.
open_risks: Telemetry latency may exceed operator reaction time, reducing feedback effectiveness.
Safety constraint gating may be overly conservative, negating efficiency gains.
Adaptive control algorithms may not be validated for all worksite conditions.
NGHE Solution may become monolithic if sub-modules are not clearly defined.
Interface contracts may be poorly defined, leading to integration issues.
Energy management logic may be diffused across multiple modules if ownership is unclear.
evidence_needed: Telemetry latency measurements and operator reaction time analysis.
Safety constraint definitions and gating latency specifications.
Validation results for adaptive control algorithms across representative worksite conditions.
Interface contract specifications (data schemas, timing requirements, error handling).
Sub-module decomposition plan for NGHE Solution.
Energy efficiency measurement methodology and acceptance criteria.
